UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user of the application, encompassing various roles such as Elderly, Caregiver, Professional, and Admin. This entity stores core personal information and role-specific relationships.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

email.description: The user's email address, used for identification (authentication is handled externally).

role.description: The role assigned to the user within the application (e.g., 'Elderly', 'Caregiver', 'Professional', 'Admin').

contactNumber.description: The user's primary contact telephone number.

profilePictureUrl.description: URL to the user's profile picture.

lastLogin.description: Timestamp of the user's last login.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

hasCompletedOnboarding.description: Indicates if the user has completed the initial onboarding flow.

mobilityNeeds.description: User-provided details about their mobility requirements.

healthAlerts.description: User-provided critical health information like allergies or chronic conditions.

accessibilitySettingsId.description: Reference to the user's accessibility settings. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:1 AccessibilitySettings)

notificationSettingsId.description: Reference to the user's notification settings. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:1 NotificationSettings)

managedPatientIds.description: References to UserProfile IDs (role: 'Elderly') that this user (role: 'Caregiver' or 'Professional') manages. (Relationship: UserProfile N:N UserProfile)

caregiverIds.description: References to UserProfile IDs (role: 'Caregiver') who manage this user (role: 'Elderly'). (Relationship: UserProfile N:N UserProfile)

professionalIds.description: References to UserProfile IDs (role: 'Professional') who manage this user (role: 'Elderly'). (Relationship: UserProfile N:N UserProfile)

id.description: Unique identifier for the AccessibilitySettings entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile this setting belongs to. (Relationship: AccessibilitySettings 1:1 UserProfile)

highContrastMode.description: Indicates if high contrast mode is enabled for the user.

reduceMotion.description: Indicates if reduced motion preference is enabled for the user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the NotificationSettings entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile this setting belongs to. (Relationship: NotificationSettings 1:1 UserProfile)

emailNotifications.description: Indicates if email notifications are enabled.

smsNotifications.description: Indicates if SMS notifications are enabled.

inAppNotifications.description: Indicates if in-app notifications are enabled.

newMessages.description: Indicates if notifications for new messages are enabled.

eventReminders.description: Indicates if notifications for event reminders are enabled.

healthAlerts.description: Indicates if notifications for health alerts are enabled.

HealthRecord.title: HealthRecord
HealthRecord.description: Represents a general health record entry for an elderly user, summarizing various health aspects.

id.description: Unique identifier for the HealthRecord entity.

patientId.description: Reference to the UserProfile (role: 'Elderly') this health record belongs to. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N HealthRecord)

recordDate.description: The date the health record entry was made.

summary.description: A brief summary of the health record entry.

notes.description: Detailed notes pertaining to the health record.

recordedByUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile (role: 'Caregiver' or 'Professional') who recorded this entry. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N HealthRecord)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the health record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the health record was last updated.

VitalsEntry.title: VitalsEntry
VitalsEntry.description: Records specific vital signs readings for an elderly user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the VitalsEntry entity.

patientId.description: Reference to the UserProfile (role: 'Elderly') for whom these vitals were recorded. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N VitalsEntry)

timestamp.description: Timestamp when the vital signs were recorded.

notes.description: Additional notes related to the vital signs entry.

recordedByUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile (role: 'Caregiver' or 'Professional') who recorded these vitals. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N VitalsEntry)

Medication.title: Medication
Medication.description: Defines a specific medication prescribed to an elderly user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Medication entity.

patientId.description: Reference to the UserProfile (role: 'Elderly') for whom this medication is prescribed. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Medication)

dosage.description: The prescribed dosage of the medication.

frequency.description: How often the medication should be taken (e.g., 'once daily', 'twice a day').

instructions.description: Specific instructions for taking the medication (e.g., 'with food', 'before bed').

prescribedByUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile (role: 'Professional') who prescribed this medication. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Medication)

startDate.description: The date when the medication regimen started.

endDate.description: The date when the medication regimen is scheduled to end (optional).

MedicationAdherence.title: MedicationAdherence
MedicationAdherence.description: Tracks the adherence of an elderly user to their prescribed medication schedule.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MedicationAdherence entity.

medicationId.description: Reference to the Medication entity this adherence record pertains to. (Relationship: Medication 1:N MedicationAdherence)

patientId.description: Reference to the UserProfile (role: 'Elderly') who is taking this medication. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N MedicationAdherence)

scheduledTime.description: The scheduled date and time for taking the medication dose.

actualTakenTime.description: The actual date and time the medication dose was taken (if applicable).

adherenceStatus.description: The status of adherence for this dose (e.g., 'Taken', 'Missed', 'Scheduled').

notes.description: Additional notes about the adherence for this dose.

recordedByUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile (role: 'Caregiver' or 'Elderly') who recorded this adherence entry. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N MedicationAdherence)

DailyCheckIn.title: DailyCheckIn
DailyCheckIn.description: Records daily self-reported health and well-being check-ins from elderly users.

id.description: Unique identifier for the DailyCheckIn entity.

patientId.description: Reference to the UserProfile (role: 'Elderly') who performed this check-in. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N DailyCheckIn)

checkInDate.description: The date of the daily check-in.

energyLevel.description: The user's self-reported energy level on a scale (e.g., 1-5).

sleptWell.description: Indicates if the user reported sleeping well.

anyConcerns.description: Any concerns or comments noted by the user during the check-in.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the daily check-in was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the daily check-in was last updated.

Conversation.title: Conversation
Conversation.description: Represents a chat conversation or thread between users.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Conversation entity.

name.description: The name of the conversation (e.g., for group chats or custom names for 1:1 chats).

participantIds.description: References to UserProfile IDs participating in this conversation. (Relationship: UserProfile N:N Conversation)

isGroupChat.description: Indicates if this is a group chat or a direct 1:1 conversation.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the conversation was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the conversation was last updated (e.g., by a new message).

Message.title: Message
Message.description: Represents an individual message sent within a conversation.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Message entity.

conversationId.description: Reference to the Conversation this message belongs to. (Relationship: Conversation 1:N Message)

senderId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who sent this message. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Message)

content.description: The text content of the message.

timestamp.description: Timestamp when the message was sent.

Event.title: Event
Event.description: Represents a scheduled event shared within the community hub.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Event entity.

description.description: A detailed description of the event.

startTime.description: The scheduled start date and time of the event.

endTime.description: The scheduled end date and time of the event.

location.description: The physical or virtual location of the event.

organizerId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who organized this event. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Event)

attendeeIds.description: References to UserProfile IDs who are attending this event. (Relationship: UserProfile N:N Event)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the event was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the event was last updated.

CommunityResource.title: CommunityResource
CommunityResource.description: Represents a shared knowledge resource within the community hub.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CommunityResource entity.

description.description: A brief description of the resource's content.

url.description: The URL pointing to the resource content.

uploadedByUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who uploaded or shared this resource. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N CommunityResource)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the resource was added to the community.

AIChatInteraction.title: AIChatInteraction
AIChatInteraction.description: Stores a log of interactions with the AI-powered chatbots and health companion.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AIChatInteraction entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who initiated the AI interaction. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N AIChatInteraction)

userRole.description: The role of the user at the time of the interaction (e.g., 'Elderly', 'Caregiver', 'Professional').

query.description: The user's input query to the AI.

response.description: The AI's generated response to the query.

timestamp.description: Timestamp when the AI interaction occurred.

aiModelUsed.description: The specific AI model that generated the response (e.g., 'Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.3', 'Google Gemini').

contextDataUsed.description: Optional: A JSON string or identifier representing contextual data used by the AI for reasoning (e.g., specific health records).

definition.description: Stores core user profile information. The document ID (`userId`) MUST be the Firebase Auth UID. Includes denormalized 'managedPatientIds', 'caregiverIds', and 'professionalIds' for relationship tracking, used to populate 'authorizedAccessors' in patient-related documents. The 'accessibilitySettingsId' and 'notificationSettingsId' fields will point to 'current' as their respective subcollection document IDs.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Auth User ID (UID).

definition.description: Stores user-specific accessibility preferences. The document ID is fixed to 'current' as it's a 1:1 relationship with the user. Includes 'userId' for authorization validation.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Auth User ID (UID) this setting belongs to.

definition.description: Stores user-specific notification preferences. The document ID is fixed to 'current' as it's a 1:1 relationship with the user. Includes 'userId' for authorization validation.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Auth User ID (UID) this setting belongs to.

definition.description: Stores general health records for an elderly patient. Includes 'patientId' for path-based ownership. CRITICAL: Includes denormalized 'authorizedAccessors' map ({ [uid: string]: boolean }) for authorization independence, containing the patient, their caregivers, and professionals. 'recordedByUserId' indicates who created the entry.

params.name: patientId
params.description: The unique Firebase Auth User ID (UID) of the Elderly patient.

params.name: healthRecordId
params.description: The unique ID of the health record entry.

definition.description: Records vital signs readings for an elderly patient. Includes 'patientId' for path-based ownership. CRITICAL: Includes denormalized 'authorizedAccessors' map ({ [uid: string]: boolean }) for authorization independence, containing the patient, their caregivers, and professionals. 'recordedByUserId' indicates who recorded the vitals.

params.name: patientId
params.description: The unique Firebase Auth User ID (UID) of the Elderly patient.

params.name: vitalsEntryId
params.description: The unique ID of the vitals entry.

definition.description: Defines prescribed medication for an elderly patient. Includes 'patientId' for path-based ownership. CRITICAL: Includes denormalized 'authorizedAccessors' map ({ [uid: string]: boolean }) for authorization independence, containing the patient, their caregivers, and professionals. 'prescribedByUserId' indicates who prescribed the medication.

params.name: patientId
params.description: The unique Firebase Auth User ID (UID) of the Elderly patient.

params.name: medicationId
params.description: The unique ID of the medication.

definition.description: Tracks medication adherence for an elderly patient. Includes 'patientId' for path-based ownership. CRITICAL: Includes denormalized 'authorizedAccessors' map ({ [uid: string]: boolean }) for authorization independence, containing the patient, their caregivers, and professionals. 'recordedByUserId' indicates who recorded the adherence (can be patient or caregiver).

params.name: patientId
params.description: The unique Firebase Auth User ID (UID) of the Elderly patient.

params.name: adherenceId
params.description: The unique ID of the medication adherence entry.

definition.description: Records daily self-reported health check-ins from elderly users. Includes 'patientId' for path-based ownership. CRITICAL: Includes denormalized 'authorizedAccessors' map ({ [uid: string]: boolean }) for authorization independence, containing the patient, their caregivers, and professionals. 'patientId' is also the 'recordedByUserId'.

params.name: patientId
params.description: The unique Firebase Auth User ID (UID) of the Elderly patient.

params.name: checkInId
params.description: The unique ID of the daily check-in entry.

definition.description: Stores logs of AI chatbot interactions, private to the user who initiated them. Includes 'userId' for path-based ownership and internal validation.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Auth User ID (UID) who initiated the AI interaction.

params.name: interactionId
params.description: The unique ID of the AI chat interaction.

definition.description: Represents a chat conversation. CRITICAL: Includes 'participantIds' (membership map) for authorization independence, defining who can access the conversation.

params.name: conversationId
params.description: The unique ID of the conversation.

definition.description: Represents an individual message within a conversation. Includes 'senderId' and 'conversationId'. CRITICAL: Denormalizes 'conversationParticipantIds' from its parent Conversation for authorization independence, allowing direct checks without `get()`.

params.name: conversationId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent conversation.

params.name: messageId
params.description: The unique ID of the message.

definition.description: Represents a scheduled community event. Includes 'organizerId' and 'attendeeIds' (membership map) for authorization independence, defining who can access/manage the event.

params.name: eventId
params.description: The unique ID of the event.

definition.description: Stores shared knowledge resources for the community. Publicly readable, but write access is restricted to the 'uploadedByUserId' or Admin users.

params.name: resourceId
params.description: The unique ID of the community resource.

definition.description: Dedicated collection for Admin role management. The existence of a document at this path for a given UID signifies Admin privileges. Document content can be minimal (e.g., just an ID or timestamp).

params.name: adminId
params.description: The unique Firebase Auth User ID (UID) of an administrator.

firestore.reasoning:
The proposed Firestore structure prioritizes Authorization Independence and Structural Segregation to ensure robust, debuggable security rules and efficient data access. 

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):** This design heavily relies on denormalization to avoid hierarchical authorization dependencies (`get()` calls in security rules):

1.  **Patient-Specific Data (Health Records, Vitals, Medications, Daily Check-Ins):** For `/users/{patientId}/...` subcollections, each document (e.g., `HealthRecord`, `VitalsEntry`) will include a denormalized `authorizedAccessors` map. This map will contain the `patientId` (the elderly user's UID) and all UIDs of their associated `Caregiver` and `Professional` users (sourced from the `caregiverIds` and `professionalIds` fields on the `patientId`'s `UserProfile`). This allows security rules to check `request.auth.uid in resource.data.authorizedAccessors` directly on the document, eliminating the need to `get()` the patient's `UserProfile`.
2.  **Messaging (`Conversation` and `Message`):**
    *   `Conversation` documents explicitly list all `participantIds`. Rules check `request.auth.uid in resource.data.participantIds`.
    *   `Message` documents, residing in a subcollection of `Conversation`, will denormalize their parent's `conversationParticipantIds` (a copy of the `participantIds` from the `Conversation` document). This ensures that rules for messages can verify sender/participant status without needing to `get()` the parent `Conversation` document.
3.  **Collaborative Events:** `Event` documents contain `organizerId` and `attendeeIds`, allowing direct authorization checks.
4.  **User-Owned Settings & AI Interactions:** `AccessibilitySettings`, `NotificationSettings`, and `AIChatInteraction` are stored as subcollections under `/users/{userId}/...`, with `userId` directly in the path and the document, ensuring clear, path-based ownership and eliminating external dependencies.
5.  **Role-Based Access (DBAC):** User roles are primarily stored in the `role` field of `UserProfile` documents. For elevated privileges, an `Admin` role is managed through a dedicated top-level collection (`/roles_admin/{adminId}`), where the *existence* of a document for a given UID grants admin privileges. This adheres to the 'Existence over Content' principle for global roles.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**

1.  **Path-Based Ownership:** For private user data (`/users/{userId}/...`), the `userId` in the path ensures that queries are automatically scoped to the authenticated user (`request.auth.uid == userId`), making `list` operations secure and efficient.
2.  **Denormalized Accessors:** For patient-specific data, the `authorizedAccessors` map in each document allows for secure and filterable `list` operations. A caregiver or professional can query for all health records where they are an `authorizedAccessor` (`collectionGroup('healthRecords').where('authorizedAccessors.' + request.auth.uid, '==', true)`). This enables robust QAPs by ensuring clients cannot request data they are not authorized to view.
3.  **Membership Models:** For `Conversations` and `Events`, the `participantIds` and `attendeeIds` fields (respectively) enable efficient `list` operations by filtering based on membership (`where('participantIds', 'array-contains', request.auth.uid)` or similar compound queries).

**Structural Segregation:** All documents within a given collection or subcollection share the same security posture. For instance, all documents under `/users/{patientId}/healthRecords` have the same access requirements based on the `authorizedAccessors` field. This prevents mixed-access data within a single collection, simplifying security rules significantly. General community resources are in `/communityResources`, separated from private data, with clear global read and restricted write policies.

This design leverages denormalization extensively, requiring robust Cloud Function triggers to maintain data consistency (e.g., updating `authorizedAccessors` when a caregiver is added to a patient's profile or updating `conversationParticipantIds` when a conversation's participants change). This upfront complexity in data management is a trade-off for simplified, secure, and performant Firestore security rules and atomic database operations.
